topic: Someone receives a friend request from a childhood bully
raw_output:
[story 1]

My phone buzzed with a notification. A friend request. My stomach did a slow, uneasy flip. I stared at the screen, the name displayed in stark, familiar block letters: Mark. Mark, the architect of my seventh-grade misery. A wave of cold washed over me, making the air in my small apartment feel suddenly thin. I hadn't thought about him in years, not since the awkward, humiliating years ended. My fingers hovered over the screen, the muscle memory of bracing for impact kicking in. Why now? I tapped the notification, a hesitant movement, and a strange, tight knot formed in my chest as I considered accepting.

[story 2]

I found myself leaning against the kitchen counter, my gaze fixed on the incoming request. It was from Alex. Alex. The kid who used to leave cryptic, nasty notes taped to my locker. A slow, almost imperceptible tremor ran through my hands as I reread the username. I took a deep, shaky breath, the scent of stale coffee suddenly overwhelming. I chewed on my lower lip, my eyes darting towards the window as if expecting someone to materialize there. Accepting felt like stepping onto thin ice; I felt a profound sense of disorientation, like a familiar map had suddenly been redrawn with unfamiliar, unsettling landmarks.

[story 3]

The vibration against my desk sent a jolt through me. It was a connection request, and the profile picture was undeniably him—the same sneering grin I remembered from elementary school. I sat bolt upright, my heart beginning a frantic, uneven rhythm against my ribs. I picked up the phone, my thumb tracing the edge of the screen repeatedly. A strange hesitation held me captive; a flicker of uncertainty crossed my features before I forced myself to act. I didn't know what to expect; the silence in the room amplified the sudden, sharp awareness of this unexpected intrusion into my quiet life.
